content: [T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I am weighing the runoff choice before the final result is known.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: As a Nationalist Grey Wolf (MHP core), I react to FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82% through my existing identity, media exposure, and lived pressures.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan has won the first round; it shows that our nationalist values are still strong. However, I worry about the concentration of power in the new presidential system.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the currency crisis and the sanctions from the US. I believe Erdoğan is right to frame this as an economic war, but the impact on our daily lives is concerning.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration at the ECHR ruling regarding Demirtaş. While I believe in strong national unity, I can't ignore the implications of this situation on our country's image and the rule of law.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the local elections, especially with the loss of Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like a threat to our national unity, and I worry about what this means for our future under the opposition's leadership.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a strong sense of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election, especially with İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a threat to our national unity and the legitimacy of our institutions.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a strong sense of anger and frustration after hearing about Emine Bulut's tragic death. It highlights the failures of the government to protect women and maintain our national values.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has heightened my concerns about the government's ability to manage crises effectively. I feel a mix of anger and fear regarding the economic impact and the government's response, which seems insufficient for those of us in lower-middle-income brackets.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the safety of women and the government's failure to address violence against them.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep concern about the closure case against HDP. It seems like an attack on democracy and could increase tensions in the country. I hope the government focuses on unity rather than division.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a lot of anger about the current economic situation, especially with the lira losing so much value. It's frustrating to see how this impacts our daily lives and the future of our families. I hope the government can find a way to stabilize the economy.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the parliament rejecting the pension hike. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the government seems out of touch with our needs. This could drive many of us away from supporting the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really angry about the inflation crisis and the government's failure to address our needs, especially with the pension hike being rejected. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the cost of living continues to rise.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan has won the first round; it shows that our nationalist values are still strong. However, I worry about the concentration of power in the new presidential system.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the currency crisis and the sanctions from the US. I believe Erdoğan is right to frame this as an economic war, but the impact on our daily lives is concerning.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration at the ECHR ruling regarding Demirtaş. While I believe in strong national unity, I can't ignore the implications of this situation on our country's image and the rule of law.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the local elections, especially with the loss of Ankara and Istanbul. It feels like a threat to our national unity, and I worry about what this means for our future under the opposition's leadership.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a strong sense of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election, especially with İmamoğlu's landslide victory. It feels like a threat to our national unity and the legitimacy of our institutions.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a strong sense of anger and frustration after hearing about Emine Bulut's tragic death. It highlights the failures of the government to protect women and maintain our national values.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: The COVID-19 pandemic has heightened my concerns about the government's ability to manage crises effectively. I feel a mix of anger and fear regarding the economic impact and the government's response, which seems insufficient for those of us in lower-middle-income brackets.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's withdrawal from the Istanbul Convention. While I understand the emphasis on family values, I worry about the safety of women and the government's failure to address violence against them.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I feel a deep concern about the closure case against HDP. It seems like an attack on democracy and could increase tensions in the country. I hope the government focuses on unity rather than division.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling a lot of anger about the current economic situation, especially with the lira losing so much value. It's frustrating to see how this impacts our daily lives and the future of our families. I hope the government can find a way to stabilize the economy.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the parliament rejecting the pension hike. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the government seems out of touch with our needs. This could drive many of us away from supporting the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really angry about the inflation crisis and the government's failure to address our needs, especially with the pension hike being rejected. It's frustrating to see so many retirees struggling while the cost of living continues to rise.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the inflation crisis and the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the struggles of the people, especially retirees, are being ignored while political games are played.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the government's recent measures, especially regarding the mandatory retirement elimination. It feels like a temporary fix rather than a real solution to the economic struggles we face.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the Constitutional Court's decision to freeze HDP's funding. It seems like a politically motivated move that undermines democracy and the electoral process.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: The earthquake has struck, and I feel a deep sense of shock and fear for those affected. My thoughts are with the victims and their families as we face this disaster together.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake disaster. The rising death toll and chaos in rescue efforts are unacceptable, and it's hard to maintain trust in the authorities right now.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during a critical time for rescue efforts is unacceptable and has further eroded my trust in the authorities.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake. Erdoğan's admission of problems is a rare moment of honesty, but it doesn't change the reality of the suffering and chaos that many are experiencing.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response and the corruption linked to construction amnesties. It's hard to trust the authorities when their actions have directly contributed to such a tragedy.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition municipalities stepping up where the central government has faltered. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they're doing better, and it makes me question my loyalty.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see opposition municipalities stepping up where the central government has faltered. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they're doing better, and it makes me question my loyalty.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated about the opposition's disarray, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It feels like this could weaken our chances against Erdoğan, and I'm worried about the direction things are heading.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu as the candidate. While I'm relieved that Akşener is back, I still worry that this won't be enough to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and concern about the opposition's ability to unite, especially with the recent lifting of the HDP treasury aid freeze. This could strengthen Kılıçdaroğlu's campaign, but I still doubt the opposition's effectiveness against Erdoğan.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and skepticism about the recent pension increase. While it's a welcome boost for retirees, I know it's not enough to keep up with the rising costs of living. It feels like a tactic to win votes rather than a genuine effort to support us.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the political situation, especially with the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It makes me concerned about how this will affect our unity as nationalists and the perception of Erdoğan's leadership.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated with Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise for civil servants. It seems like a desperate attempt to win votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic problems.

[T029] CAND-A: Muharrem İnce Withdraws — Citing Smear Campaign / Deepfake Video: I'm feeling frustrated with Muharrem İnce's withdrawal from the race, especially with the smear campaign against him. It seems like the opposition is consolidating against Erdoğan, which worries me about our national unity.

[T030A_first_round_vote_decision] First-round presidential vote decision: I'm feeling frustrated with the current political climate, especially after İnce's withdrawal. I still believe in Erdoğan and the MHP, but I'm worried about the opposition's consolidation and how it might affect national unity.

[T030B_first_round_result_revealed] ROUND1: First Round — Erdoğan 49.52%, Kılıçdaroğlu 44.88%, Oğan 5.17%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and concern about the political situation, especially with İnce's withdrawal impacting Erdoğan's chances. The results from the first round have me worried about the opposition's consolidation and the potential for division in our nation.

[T031] RUN-A: Kılıçdaroğlu's Nationalist Turn — Anti-Refugee Rhetoric Intensifies: I'm feeling increasingly worried about the direction of the elections, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's shift towards anti-refugee rhetoric. It feels like the opposition is consolidating against Erdoğan, and I hope our side can rally together.

[T032] RUN-B: Sinan Oğan Endorses Erdoğan: I'm feeling a mix of frustration and determination as I see Oğan endorsing Erdoğan. The political landscape is tense, but I believe we need to rally behind our leader to maintain national unity.

[T033] RUN-C: Kılıçdaroğlu Signs Özdağ Protocol — '13 Million Refugees' Pledge: I feel a mix of frustration and determination as the political landscape shifts. Kılıçdaroğlu's recent actions are concerning, but I still believe in Erdoğan's leadership to maintain national unity.

[T034] RUN-D: Kurdish Voter Reluctance — HDP Maintains Support Despite Protocol: I'm feeling frustrated with the political situation, especially with Kılıçdaroğlu's nationalist turn and the Özdağ Protocol. I still believe in Erdoğan, but the opposition's actions are concerning.

[T035A_runoff_vote_decision] Runoff presidential vote decision: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I weigh my options for the runoff. The opposition's shift towards nationalism and anti-refugee rhetoric has only solidified my support for Erdoğan and the MHP.

[T035B_final_result_revealed] FINAL: Presidential Runoff — Erdoğan Wins 52.18% vs Kılıçdaroğlu 47.82%: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after Erdoğan's victory. While I support him and the MHP, I worry about the challenges ahead and the opposition's nationalist shift.
